steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts Toffler’s specific failed predictions (paper clothes, underwater cities) with his fundamental claim — that rapid change causes disorientation and atomization — which the phrase “our age of disequilibrium and fragmentation” confirms came true. The blank must describe a claim that proved accurate before the fact.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — prescient: means having foresight or knowing what would happen before it did. Toffler predicted disorientation and atomization; we now live in an age of disequilibrium and fragmentation. The prediction proved accurate. ✓
  • Choice B — articulate: means clearly expressed. Whether Toffler stated his claim well says nothing about whether it turned out to be right, which is what the passage is evaluating. ✗
  • Choice C — iconoclastic: means attacking established beliefs or conventions. The passage is judging the claim’s accuracy, not its unconventionality. ✗
  • Choice D — equivocal: means ambiguous or open to two interpretations. The passage presents Toffler’s claim as validated, not unclear. ✗
That matches Choice A — prescient.
